1.notes: Par 4 opener. Donald Ross design with classic bunkering. Generous fairway with strategic greenside bunkers.

2.notes: Long par 4 with Ross-style undulating green. 84 original bunkers on course.

3.notes: Par 3 with Ross's trademark crowned green. Bunkers guard front and sides.

4.notes: Dogleg left par 4. Classic Ross strategy - favor right side off tee for better angle.

5.notes: Reachable par 5 for long hitters. Water comes into play on layup.

6.notes: Medium par 4 with Ross's strategic bunkering. Green slopes from back to front.

7.notes: Strategic par 4 with cross bunkers. Classic Ross risk/reward off tee.

8.notes: Par 3 with challenging green complex. Bunkers surround the putting surface.

9.notes: Finishing hole for front nine. Strategic bunkering requires accurate approach.

10.notes: Long par 4 to start back nine. Ross's signature crowned green.

11.notes: Par 5 with water in play. Classic Ross bunkering with strategic angles.

12.notes: Medium par 4 with undulating Ross green. Pin placement crucial.

13.notes: Long par 3 with Ross's strategic bunkering. Club selection critical.

14.notes: Strategic par 4 with Ross's classic bunkering pattern. Right side preferred off tee.

15.notes: Medium par 4. Strategic bunker placement requires accurate tee shot.

16.notes: Challenging par 3 over water. Ross's crowned green punishes misses.

17.notes: Long par 5 with water hazard. Three-shot hole for most players.

18.notes: Finishing hole. Classic Ross design requires accurate approach to crowned green.

1.notes: Opening par 3. Davis Love III renovation 2023. Challenging start demands accuracy.

2.notes: Long par 4. Downhill tee shot to generous landing area. Approach is key.

3.notes: Short par 3. Pin placement varies difficulty significantly.

4.notes: Short par 4. Risk/reward off tee - big hitters can challenge green.

5.notes: Long demanding par 4. Accuracy off tee crucial for approach angle.

6.notes: Downhill par 3 to well-protected green. Club selection tricky with elevation.

7.notes: Long par 4 with dogleg. Water on left threatens aggressive plays.

8.notes: Par 5 reachable for long hitters. Creek crosses fairway at 280.

9.notes: Finishing hole front nine. Long uphill par 4 to elevated green.

10.notes: Long par 4 to start back nine. Right side of fairway opens up green.

11.notes: Short par 4. Scoring opportunity with accurate approach.

12.notes: Long par 5. Three-shot hole with water threatening second shot.

13.notes: Long demanding par 4. Dogleg left requires accurate drive placement.

14.notes: Long par 3 over water. Club selection critical with elevation change.

15.notes: Long par 5 finishing stretch begins. Water left threatens layup.

16.notes: Medium par 4 with strategic bunkering. Green angled away from approach.

17.notes: Short par 3. Deceptively difficult with tricky green complex.

18.notes: Finishing hole. Long uphill par 4 to amphitheater green.

1.notes: Opening par 4. Heavy left-side bunkering and large bunker right off tee. Small green slopes back to front. Links-style course.

2.notes: Long par 4, slight dogleg right. Trees left and right. Plays into prevailing wind. Notre Dame campus views from approach.

3.notes: Slight dogleg right with large pond right of green. Left side of fairway gives best approach angle. Fairway slopes left to right.

4.notes: Shortest hole on course. Small undulating green guarded by large bunkers front, left, and right. Finding correct level crucial.

5.notes: Par 5 reachable in two with wind. Monstrous bunker right, three bunkers left off tee. Small pot bunker front of green, large bunker left.

6.notes: Longest par 4. Slight dogleg right with deep bunkers right and large bunkers front of green. Run-up option available. 2019 Senior Open 18th hole.

7.notes: Risk/reward par 4. Set of bunkers forces decision - lay up short or take them on. Large green with severe back to front slope.

8.notes: Tree-lined par 4 requiring carry over ravine and bunkers. False front on rectangular green. Plays into wind. Generous landing area.

9.notes: Deceptive par 3. Large bunker appears greenside but is 20 yards short. Take half club more. Aim at clubhouse chimney.

10.notes: Par 5 with options. Drive carrying crest allows reaching in two. Creek front and left of green. Massive false front. Bail out right of green.

11.notes: Longest par 3, largest green on course. Bunkers line fairway and border green. False front demands carrying shot onto surface.

12.notes: Long difficult par 4. Trees both sides, bunkers in landing area. Par is very good score. Requires big tee shot.

13.notes: Long dogleg left. Bunkers guard left side. Right side of fairway gives best approach angle. Green accepts running approach.

14.notes: Last par 3. Well-bunkered green with large bunker left and hidden bunker right. False front demands carrying shot. Condoleezza Rice hole-in-one here.

15.notes: Three bunkers right, two large bunkers left frame landing zone. T-shaped green with large greenside bunkers. Carry to wide part of fairway.

16.notes: Signature hole. Tee shot over Juday Creek, heavy bunkering left. Short uphill approach to severely undulating green with front pot bunker. 2019 Senior Open opening hole.

17.notes: Last par 5. Elevated tee to fairway bordered by pond/wetlands on left. Uphill second shot to one of more narrow, small greens. Miss short for bogey.

18.notes: Strong finishing hole. Dogleg left requires carry over Juday Creek. Waste area runs entire left side. Large cottonwood tree blocks right approach. Bill Coore/Ben Crenshaw restored after 2016 flood.
